title: 10 best herbal tinctures for pimples
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for pimples are concentrated extracts of healing herbs, which are commonly used in natural skincare to treat acne. They are usually made by soaking plant material in alcohol or vinegar to extract the beneficial compounds. Some popular herbal tinctures for pimples include tea tree, lavender, and echinacea. These natural remedies help with pimples by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting skin healing. By incorporating herbal tinctures into a skincare routine, individuals can experience clearer, healthier skin, and improved self-confidence. For example, a person prone to breakouts may use a tea tree tincture to disinfect pores and reduce redness, leading to a significant decrease in pimples and an overall improvement in their complexion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with pimples because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by pimples. The herb's astringent qualities can help dry out excess oil and tighten pores, preventing further breakouts. Additionally, yarrow tincture contains salicylic acid, a well-known ingredient for treating acne, which can exfoliate the skin, unclog pores, and promote skin cell turnover. By using yarrow tincture regularly, you can help maintain clear, healthy-looking skin and reduce the appearance of pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties help fight the bacteria that cause pimples, as it contains active compounds like azulenes and lactones that inhibit bacterial growth.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent properties help shrink and tighten pores, reducing the likelihood of new pimples, due to the tannins present in the plant.
Wound-healing: Yarrow tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is beneficial for pimples as it helps minimize scarring and promotes healthy skin cell growth.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with pimples, as it contains active compounds like salicylic acid and volatile oils that have pain-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Chop the yarrow into small pieces and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the yarrow, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pimple redness and swelling.
Leaves: Contain antiseptic and astringent qualities, helping to dry out excess oil and kill bacteria that can cause pimples.
Seeds: Have high levels of azulene, which reduces inflammation and promotes healing of pimples.
Stems: Contain tannins that have an astringent effect, helping to tighten pores and reduce the occurrence of pimples.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with pimples because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples. Burdock contains phenolic acids, quercetin, and luteolin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, a bacterium that contributes to the development of pimples. Additionally, burdock has blood purifying properties that can help eliminate toxins from the body, reducing the likelihood of pimple outbreaks. By promoting skin healing and reducing inflammation, burdock tincture can help clear up pimples and improve overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for pimples as it lessens redness and swelling. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, essential oils, and quercetin.

Antibacterial: Burdock tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria, including Propionibacterium acnes, which contributes to pimple formation. The antibacterial properties come from its phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic and caffeic acids.

Blood purification: Burdock tincture helps detoxify the blood, eliminating waste and toxins that can lead to pimples. This is due to its diuretic and diaphoretic effects, as well as the presence of arctiin and lignans.

Hormone balancing: Burdock tincture helps regulate hormones, which is crucial for pimples since hormonal imbalances can trigger acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyacetylenes and tannins.

Anti-oxidative: Burdock tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to pimple formation. The antioxidant effects are attributed to its phenolic acids, quercetin, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried burdock root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the burdock root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage (e.g. 1-2 dropperfuls up to three times a day) and store in a cool, dark place.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for pimples due to their blood purifying properties.
Seeds: Burdock seeds are rich in essential fatty acids and are used in tinctures to help reduce inflammation and redness associated with pimples.
Leaves: Burdock leaves have antibacterial properties and are used in tinctures to help kill acne-causing bacteria.
Rhyzomes: Burdock rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that are used in tinctures to reduce swelling and irritation caused by pimples.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tincture helps with pimples because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples. The herb's active compounds, such as taraxacin, help to regulate sebum production, which can prevent clogged pores and breakouts. Additionally, daisy tincture has been shown to promote skin cell renewal, which can speed up the healing process and reduce the appearance of acne scars. Its gentle and natural formula makes it an effective and safe option for those looking for a natural remedy for pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness and swelling of pimples, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial property of daisy tincture combats acne-causing bacteria, preventing the formation and spread of pimples, due to its content of pyrethrins and tannins.
Astringent: Daisy tincture's astringent property helps dry out excess oil and tighten pores, minimizing the appearance of pimples, as a result of its tannin content.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of daisy tincture protects skin from oxidative stress and promotes skin cell regeneration, aiding in the healing of pimples, because of its flavonoid and phenolic acid content.
Soothing: Daisy tincture's soothing property alleviates skin irritation and calms inflamed skin, reducing the discomfort associated with pimples, due to its flavonoid and essential oil content.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh daisy flowers, fill a clean jar up to 3/4 with them, then add 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged.
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Discard the used flowers and store the liquid in a cool, dark place; the tincture should last for several years.
Apply the daisy tincture to pimples with a cotton swab, using a small amount as needed, and avoid getting it in your eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, daisy flowers can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Leaves: Containing anti-bacterial and astringent properties, daisy leaves can help combat acne-causing bacteria and tighten pores.
Buds: Packed with antioxidants, daisy buds can help protect skin from damage and promote healing of pimples.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, daisy seeds can help soothe irritated skin and support overall skin health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with pimples because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and infection associated with pimples. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help regulate sebum production, preventing clogged pores and future breakouts. Additionally, pot marigold tincture has been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can aid in the faster recovery of damaged skin caused by pimples. Its soothing and calming effects can also decrease the appearance of scars and promote an even skin tone.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation associated with pimples, which is important because inflammation can lead to redness, swelling, and pain. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenoids in pot marigold.

Antibacterial: The tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria that can contribute to the development of pimples, making it important for preventing and treating acne. The active constituents responsible for this property include the alkaloids, saponins, and tannins found in pot marigold.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is beneficial for pimples as it helps to speed up the recovery process and reduces the likelihood of scarring. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, triterpenoids, and essential oils in pot marigold.

Astringent: The tincture has astringent properties that help to tighten and tone the skin, which is important for reducing the appearance of pimples and preventing new ones from forming. This property is due to the presence of tannins in pot marigold.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture contains antioxidants that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of pimples. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids found in pot marigold.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh pot marigold flowers and leaves, rinse them thoroughly, and let them dry.
Chop the dried herbs finely and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters.
Pour 100-proof vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely submerged, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use a dropper to apply the tincture directly to pimples or mix it with a carrier oil for a facial treatment.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids, which have antimicrobial and astringent properties, aiding in the healing of pimples and preventing further breakouts.
Seeds: Extracted oil from the seeds has been found to have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to eliminate acne-causing bacteria and reducing pimple size.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with pimples because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce redness and swelling of the skin. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, which contains high levels of catechins, a type of antioxidant that helps to reduce the production of sebum, an oily substance that can clog pores and lead to pimples. Additionally, white tea has antibacterial properties that can help to kill the bacteria that cause acne, making it an effective natural remedy for treating and preventing pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling of pimples, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of acne-causing bacteria, due to the catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).
Antioxidant: White tea tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect skin cells from damage and reduces oxidative stress, which can worsen pimples.
Astringent: The astringent properties of white tea tincture help to dry out excess oil on the skin, reducing the likelihood of pore clogging, thanks to its tannin content.
Soothing: White tea tincture's soothing properties help to calm irritated skin, reducing the appearance of pimples, as a result of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant constituents.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried organic white tea leaves and 1/2 cup of dried organic pimple-fighting herbs (such as calendula, lavender, and yarrow) into small pieces.
Combine the chopped herbs and 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent herbs and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished white tea tincture in a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and redness caused by pimples.
Buds: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating acne and reducing pimple size.
Flowers: Contain antiseptic and astringent properties that help cleanse and tighten pores, preventing the formation of pimples.
Stems: May have antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate acne-causing bacteria.
Roots: Can have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and heal skin affected by pimples.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cannabis tincture helps with pimples because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling of the skin. Additionally, cannabis tincture has been found to have antibacterial properties that can help combat the bacteria that can cause acne. The cannabinoids in cannabis tincture, such as CBD, can also help regulate the production of sebum, an oily substance that can contribute to the development of pimples. By reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and regulating sebum production, cannabis tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of pimples and improve overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cannabis tincture can reduce inflammation associated with pimples, helping to alleviate redness, swelling, and pain. This property is important for pimples as inflammation can worsen their appearance and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include cannabinoids such as CBD and THC.

Antibacterial: Cannabis tincture has antibacterial properties that can help kill bacteria responsible for acne, including Propionibacterium acnes. This property is important for pimples as bacteria can contribute to their formation and exacerbate symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpenes such as α-pinene and β-caryophyllene.

Sebum regulation: Cannabis tincture can help regulate sebum production, reducing the amount of oil on the skin and preventing clogged pores. This property is important for pimples as excessive sebum production can lead to their formation. The active constituents responsible for this property include cannabinoids such as CBD.

Anti-oxidative: Cannabis tincture has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the signs of aging and improving overall skin health. This property is important for pimples as oxidative stress can contribute to their formation. The active constituents responsible for this property include cannabinoids such as CBD and flavonoids such as quercetin.

Analgesic: Cannabis tincture can help alleviate pain associated with pimples, reducing discomfort and improving quality of life. This property is important for pimples as they can cause physical discomfort and embarrassment. The active constituents responsible for this property include cannabinoids such as THC and CBD.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of cannabis flower and 2 ounces of herbs known for treating pimples, such as lavender, chamomile, and calendula, then combine in a large jar.
Pour 2 cups of high-proof alcohol, like vodka or grain alcohol, over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged, then secure the lid and store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks.
Shake the jar gently every few days to help the herbs release their medicinal properties.
After the recommended steeping time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean jar or bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the finished tincture in a cool, dark place and apply a few drops to the affected area as needed, taking care to avoid the eyes and mouth.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of cannabinoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling of pimples.
Leaves: Rich in cannabinoids and flavonoids, which have antibacterial and soothing effects, helping to alleviate irritation and prevent acne-causing bacteria.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, which can help regulate sebum production, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores and pimples.
Buds: Packed with therapeutic compounds, including cannabigerolic acid (CBGA), which has been shown to have potential antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, aiding in the treatment of pimples.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with pimples because it contains high levels of silica, a mineral that supports collagen production and promotes skin health. This herb has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can reduce redness, swelling, and infection associated with pimples. Additionally, horsetail tincture may help regulate sebum production, preventing clogged pores and future breakouts. Its astringent properties can help tighten pores, giving the skin a smoother and more even appearance. Overall, horsetail tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for managing pimples and promoting clearer, healthier skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tincture's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes, due to the presence of antioxidant compounds like silica and tannins.

Astringent: Horsetail tincture's astringent properties help to dry out excess oil and tighten pores, reducing the likelihood of future pimples, due to the presence of silicic acid and other organic acids.

Wound healing: Horsetail tincture promotes wound healing, which can help to minimize the appearance of pimples and prevent scarring, thanks to the presence of bioactive compounds like equisetonin and saponins.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tincture's antioxidant properties protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of pimples, due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried horsetail herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or other high-proof alcohol over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding the spent herbs.
Label the bottle with the name, dosage, and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Recommended dosage is 1-2 mL, 2-3 times daily.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain silica and other minerals that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of pimples.
Stems: Horsetail stems are rich in antioxidants and have astringent properties that can help to dry out pimples and reduce redness.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe irritated skin and reduce the appearance of pimples.
Flowers: While horsetail is not known to produce flowers, if it did, they would likely contain beneficial compounds that could be used to make medicinal tinctures for pimples.

Seeds: Horsetail seeds are not typically used in herbal medicine, but if they were, they could potentially provide additional nutrients and compounds that could help to reduce the severity of pimples.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with pimples because it has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by pimples. It contains tannins, which have astringent qualities that can help dry out excess oil on the skin, preventing the clogged pores that lead to pimples. Additionally, eyebright tincture contains flavonoids, which are antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of pimples. Overall, eyebright tincture can help soothe and heal pimples while preventing new ones from forming.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of eyebright tincture combats acne-causing bacteria, preventing the infection and inflammation of pimples, due to the presence of active compounds like aucubin, euphrasyntin A, and euphrasyntin B.
Astringent: Eyebright tincture's astringent property helps shrink and tighten pimple tissue, reducing the size and appearance of blemishes, by the action of tannins and other polyphenolic compounds.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of eyebright tincture helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the likelihood of pimples and promoting overall skin health, attributed to the presence of various flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Soothing: Eyebright tincture's soothing property alleviates skin irritation and itchiness, providing relief for inflamed and sensitive pimples, due to its calming constituents such as saponins, iridoids, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried eyebright herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solid material.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage (typically 20-30 drops up to three times a day) and store it in a cool, dark place.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce redness and fight acne-causing bacteria.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, promoting skin cell regeneration and reducing the appearance of pimples and scars.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with potent antimicrobial and astringent properties, helping to control sebum production and tighten pores.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with pimples because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and infection associated with pimples. It also contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant effects that can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Additionally, fennel tincture can help regulate oil production in the skin, which can help prevent the clogged pores that lead to pimples. The combination of these properties makes fennel tincture a valuable tool in managing and preventing pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, thanks to compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of fennel tincture combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing the infection and inflammation that lead to pimples, due to the presence of volatile oils such as trans-anethole and fenchone.

Antioxidant: Fennel tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the likelihood of pimples, as a result of the action of compounds like rosmarinic acid and vitamin C.
Astringent: Fennel tincture's astringent properties help shrink and tighten pores, reducing the likelihood of pore clogging and the formation of pimples, owing to the tannins present in fennel.

Phytoestrogenic: Fennel tincture's phytoestrogenic properties can help balance hormones, which can contribute to the development of pimples, particularly in women, by mimicking the effects of estrogen in the body.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried fennel seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum over the fennel seeds until they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the solids and keeping the liquid.
Store the final fennel tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used in medicinal tinctures for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating pimples and preventing future breakouts.
Leaves: Fennel leaves contain high levels of vitamin C, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in pimples.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful in treating pimples and preventing infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with pimples because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples. Additionally, licorice tincture can help regulate the production of sebum, an oily substance that can clog pores and lead to pimples. By reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and regulating sebum production, licorice tincture can help clear up pimples and promote healthy, clear skin. However, it is important to note that while licorice tincture can be effective for treating pimples, it should not be used as a substitute for a regular skincare routine or medical treatment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture helps reduce inflammation associated with pimples, soothing the skin and alleviating redness. This property is due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tincture inhibits the growth of acne-causing bacteria, preventing the formation and spread of pimples. The key active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and coumarins.

Antioxidant: Licorice tincture protects skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the likelihood of pimples. The antioxidant property comes from the active constituents liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin.

Melanin inhibition: Licorice tincture decreases melanin production, which can help reduce post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation and dark spots left by pimples. The active constituent glabridin is responsible for this property.
Sebum regulation: Licorice tincture helps balance sebum production, preventing excess oil that can lead to pimples. This property is attributed to the active constituent glycyrrhetinic acid.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply a few drops to a cotton ball and gently dab on pimples as needed.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds such as glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria that cause pimples.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain essential oils and fatty acids, which can help soothe irritated skin and reduce the appearance of pimples.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain isoflavonoids, which have been shown to have estrogenic effects that can help regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to pimples.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help treat and prevent pimples.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat pimples with herbal tinctures is by using a solution of diluted Echinacea or tea tree oil. These natural remedies have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce redness, fight bacteria, and promote healing. Mix a few drops of the chosen tincture with water or a carrier oil and apply it directly to the pimple using a clean cotton swab. Repeat this process 1-2 times a day until the pimple disappears. Always remember to perform a patch test first to ensure you are not sensitive or allergic to the tincture.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pimples that are treated with herbal tinctures include acne, blackheads, and whiteheads. These tinctures often contain ingredients such as tea tree oil, calendula, and echinacea, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Tea tree oil, for example, is known to kill bacteria that cause acne, while calendula and echinacea can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Other herbs that may be used in tinctures for treating pimple-like ailments include burdock root, which can help detoxify the skin, and lavender, which has calming and soothing effects. However, it's important to note that herbal tinctures can interact with other medications and may not be suitable for everyone, so it's always best to consult with a healthcare provider before trying them.
